brief_justification: Paper A is an estimation/inequality method: it proves sharp lower-bound (Hardy) constants on the lattice by Fourier reduction to the torus and an iterative ground-state-representation plus weighted Bochner identity scheme. Paper B is an algebraic solution-generating method: it builds a quasiPfaffian and a Sylvester-Moutard (Darboux-type) transformation to produce new exact solutions of integrable PDEs. Bounding an optimal constant versus generating solutions of nonlinear equations are different mechanisms with no role-for-role correspondence; the only overlap (both invoke a named 'classical identity') is superficial.
